<result>All edits confirmed. Here is the final report.

## Summary

Architectural cutover spec surgery complete. RBSDI deleted, `rbtgo_depot_inscribe` quoin fully retired (all three MCM parts), and ANCHOR/reliquary/conclave prose finalized across the two RBS files. All linked terms I introduced (`rbtga_lode`, `rbtga_touchmark`, `rbtgo_lode_conclave`, `rbst_locator`, `gar_lodes_namespace`, `rbtgo_lode_ensconce`) verified as defined quoins.

### Per-file changes

**`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDI-depot_inscribe.adoc`** — DELETED (the retired reliquary-inscribe operation body).

**`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`**
- Task B — `rbtgo_depot_inscribe` quoin fully removed: mapping-section attribute ref (`:rbtgo_depot_inscribe: <<...,rbfl_inscribe>>`), the `[[rbtgo_depot_inscribe]]` operation section + its `include::RBSDI-depot_inscribe.adoc[]`. Both surviving usages re-pointed to `{rbtgo_lode_conclave}`: `rbf_fact_reliquary` ("Written by … on successful Cloud Build submission") and `rbst_reliquary_stamp` ("Mints host-side at … time").
- Task A — ANCHOR/election finalization: `rbrv_image_anchor` bole-locator bullet now states the actual locator `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»:rbi_bole` written by the derived-pull election at ordain time (reading the chained touchmark fact), with the fingerprint demoted to a sibling member tag. Dropped enshrine residue tied to base anchors: in-pool preflight "enshrined base anchors" → "bole {rbtga_lode} base anchors"; `rbsk_pinning_boundary` "the enshrined anchors" → "the bole {rbtga_lode} base anchors" and "the inscribe/conclave ceremony" → "the conclave ceremony … into one {rbtga_lode}"; dropped retired-enshrine from the ordain-path job list and the GCB-step-context list.
- Task C — `rbtgo_director_yoke` now "Yokes a reliquary touchmark … validates the conclave {rbtga_lode} touchmark"; `rbrv_reliquary` "datestamp" → "touchmark identifying the conclave {rbtga_lode}"; `rbst_reliquary_stamp` reconciled to the conclave Lode (`rbi_ld/<touchmark>` with `:rbi_<tool>` member tags, mints at conclave time); `rbst_locator` format example replaced `rbi_rq/«STAMP»/syft` and `rbi_es/«ANCHOR»` with conclave member-locator `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»:rbi_syft` and bole `…:rbi_bole`; preflight coverage "5 of 6 reliquary tools" → "6 of 7 reliquary cohort member tags" (added gcrane), remediation "re-inscribe" → "re-conclave", "each missed item" → "each missed member tag".

**`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRV-RegimeVessel.adoc`**
- `rbrv_egress_mode`: "Inscribe and enshrine always use tether" → "Capture operations — conclave and the rest of the {rbtga_lode} family — always use tether (upstream fetch requires public egress)".
- `rbrv_reliquary`: "datestamp" → "touchmark identifying the conclave {rbtga_lode}".
- `rbrv_image_anchor`: Format changed from the bare fingerprint `{sanitized-origin}-{10-char-sha256}` to the full `package-path:tag` {rbst_locator} `{gar_lodes_namespace}/«TOUCHMARK»:rbi_bole` (fingerprint demoted to sibling member tag); added "at ordain time".

### Dangling-reference audit

Clean within my three editable files: zero `rbtgo_depot_inscribe`, `rbfl_inscribe`, or `RBSDI` references remain; no orphaned mapping attr-ref; all introduced linked terms resolve to defined RBS0 quoins.

**UNRESOLVED — outside my editable scope, needs your sweep:**
- **`RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc:19`** — a live `{rbtgo_depot_inscribe}` linked-term usage ("recovery is nuclear: re-run {rbtgo_depot_inscribe}…"). This is now a **dangling xref** (no definition). Re-point to `{rbtgo_lode_conclave}`. I could not touch RBSIJ (not in my three-file allowlist).
- **Broad retired-`enshrine`/`rbi_es` residue** (~30 hits) lives in spec files I'm not permitted to edit — chiefly `RBSCB-CloudBuildPosture.adoc` (inscribe/enshrine pool-routing table + dedup notes), `RBSHR-HorizonRoadmap.adoc` (enshrine→Lode roadmap), `RBSIA-image_audit.adoc` (whole Enshrinements-Audit domain + `gar_enshrines_namespace` root), `RBSIW`/`RBSIJ` (locator-domain-blind prose listing `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»`), `RBSAV-ark_vouch.adoc:209` ("GAR-mirrored via enshrine"), `RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc:4` (lists "enshrine" as a consuming op), `RBSAC`, `RBSAK`, `RBSCL`, `RBSCJ`, `RBSDE`, `RBSIP`, `RBSRT`. Flagging for your follow-up cutover pass.
- The **`gar_enshrines_namespace` quoin** (RBS0:203, 1885-1887) is intentionally LEFT IN PLACE — it is still actively referenced by RBSIA/RBSIW/RBSIJ/RBSDE; deleting it would cascade dangles across those out-of-scope files. RBS0:2145 already documents it "fold[ing] into" `rbi_ld` as kinds land.

### Quoins flagged for your review (not deleted — deletion risks dangles)
- **`rbst_reliquary_stamp`** — now arguably redundant with `rbst_touchmark` (both `r\d{12}`; the reliquary kind is now just a touchmarked conclave Lode). Per your instruction I updated its prose rather than deleting it. It is still referenced by the `rbrv_reliquary`/yoke chain; consider whether to collapse it into `rbst_touchmark` in a later pass.

No quoin I expected to exist was missing — `rbtgo_lode_conclave`, `gar_lodes_namespace`, `rbtga_lode`, `rbtga_touchmark`, and `rbst_locator` all exist; I did not invent any. No commits or builds were run.</result>

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger - kindle entry: the single rbfl inclusion-guard and
# kindle/sentinel, sourcing the Foundry Core entry (rbfck_) and the guard-free body
# clusters (rbfli_ inscribe, rbfly_ yoke, rbfld_ delete, rbfln_ inventory, rbflw_
# wrest). The readonly ZRBFL_* constants the kindle sets are read globally.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBFL_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBFL_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core entry and the guard-free body clusters
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfli_Inscribe.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfly_Yoke.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfld_Delete.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfln_Inventory.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbflw_Wrest.sh"

# Cross-source from the rbld (Lode) family: abjure dispatches its GAR package
# delete cloud-side through the build-assembly spine and the shared cloud-delete
# body, exactly as banish does (the cinch-blessed narrow cross into made-side
# delete). Both carry their own inclusion guards, so this single-source-per-
# process reach raises no double-source; the spine reads only rbfc-level state
# (zrbfc_sentinel), which this process kindles.
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rblds_Spine.sh"
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbldd_Delete.sh"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfl_*)

zrbfl_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFL_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Validate Foundry Core is kindled'
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Verify Director RBRA file'
  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"

  buc_log_args 'RBGJI inscribe step scripts (same Tools directory)'
  local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
  readonly ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgji"
  test -d "${ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJI steps directory not found: ${ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR}"

  buc_log_args 'Define delete operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_delete_"

  buc_log_args 'Define reliquary inscribe operation file prefix'
  readonly ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_reliquary_"

  readonly ZRBFL_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfl_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBFL_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfl not kindled - call zrbfl_kindle first"
}

# eof

rbfl_rekon_reliquary() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  local -r z_stamp="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "List tool images present under a reliquary stamp's GAR subtree"
  buc_doc_param "stamp" "Reliquary datestamp (e.g., r260327172456)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_stamp}" || buc_die "Usage: rbw-irr <stamp>"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  buc_step "Enumerating tool images under ${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}/"
  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}"

  # Filter the full reliquary enumeration to rows for this stamp.
  local z_found=""
  local z_line=""
  local z_s=""
  local z_t=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
    z_s="${z_line%% *}"
    z_t="${z_line#* }"
    if test "${z_s}" = "${z_stamp}"; then
      z_found="${z_found}${z_found:+ }${z_t}"
    fi
  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"

  test -n "${z_found}" || buc_die "Reliquary stamp not found: ${z_stamp}"

  echo ""
  printf "  %-10s  %-6s  %s\n" "TOOL" "EXISTS" "PACKAGE-PATH"
  printf "  %-10s  %-6s  %s\n" "----------" "------" "------------"

  local z_canon=""
  local z_mark=""
  local z_path=""
  for z_canon in \
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}" \
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}" \
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}" \
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}" \
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}" \
    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}"; do
    z_mark="no"
    case " ${z_found} " in
      *" ${z_canon} "*) z_mark="yes" ;;
    esac
    if test "${z_mark}" = "yes"; then
      z_path="${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}/${z_canon}"
    else
      z_path="(absent)"
    fi
    printf "  %-10s  %-6s  %s\n" "${z_canon}" "${z_mark}" "${z_path}"
  done

  echo ""
  buc_success "Rekon complete for ${z_stamp}"
}

input.old_string:
rbfl_audit_reliquaries() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Audit reliquaries — list all reliquary stamps in registry"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  buc_step "Enumerating reliquaries under ${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/"
  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}"

  if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
    buc_info "No reliquaries found under ${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/"
    buc_success "Audit complete — 0 reliquaries"
    return 0
  fi

  echo ""
  printf "  %s\n" "RELIQUARY-STAMP"
  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"

  local z_count=0
  local z_prev=""
  local z_line=""
  local z_s=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
    z_s="${z_line%% *}"
    test -n "${z_s}" || continue
    if test "${z_s}" != "${z_prev}"; then
      printf "  %s\n" "${z_s}"
      z_count=$(( z_count + 1 ))
      z_prev="${z_s}"
    fi
  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"

  echo ""
  buc_info "Total reliquaries: ${z_count}"
  buc_success "Audit complete"
}

=== zipper verb-const generation (RBTDGC_VERB_*) ===
143:  # Primary layer: the type-blind, path-polymorphic raw trio on bare verb
146:  # namespace; whole-package delete stays with the semantic verbs (banish/
147:  # abjure). The bare verb letters il/iw/iJ are the FINAL-FORM colophons; iw
153:  # cutover. Their verbs: rekon (member-list), audit (catalog-list), wrest
250:# Rust const projection (colophons → RBTDGC_)
252:# rbz_emit_consts() - Emit the complete generated RBTDGC_ consts file to stdout.

256:# projection prefixes — const name is RBTDGC_ + the enroll varname with its
265:  buz_emit_colophon_consts "RBTDGC_" "RBZ_" || buc_die "rbz_emit_consts: colophon emit failed"
270:# rbz_generate_consts() - Write the colophon const file, only on difference.

287:    cp "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" || buc_die "rbz_generate_consts: failed to write ${z_target}"

# Reliquary Tool Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Canonical tool names under rbi_rq/<date>/. Authoritative
  # manifest lives in rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh.
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD="gcloud"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER="docker"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE="alpine"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT="syft"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT="binfmt"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO="skopeo"
  # gcrane joins the cohort so sealed-reliquary-consuming captures (bole/wsl)
  # resolve a PINNED gcrane builder from the reliquary, never the floating gcr.io
  # bootstrap (the supply-chain pinning boundary — RBS0 rbsk_pinning_boundary,
  # RBSCB). Mirrored as the :debug variant (busybox shell) so the resolved builder
  # carries the orchestration shell its capture steps need.
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCRANE="gcrane"

33	
34	# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — authoritative co-versioned set for
35	# GCB step execution; mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim (the
36	# two coexist during the reliquary cutover). gcloud, docker, and gcrane are
37	# Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane rides the :debug variant
38	# so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell its capture steps need.
39	# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
40	# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
41	# so the cohort cannot be expressed as the `MANIFEST=( … )` array rbgji01 (running
42	# under bash) still uses; a while-read over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form,
43	# matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.
44

file.content:
# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — authoritative co-versioned set for
# GCB step execution; mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim (the
# two coexist during the reliquary cutover). gcloud, docker, and gcrane are
# Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane rides the :debug variant
# so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell its capture steps need.
# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
# so the cohort cannot be expressed as the `MANIFEST=( … )` array rbgji01 (running
# under bash) still uses; a while-read over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form,
# matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_image_rekon}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation reports which canonical members are present under a
5	hallmark or reliquary subtree in
6	{rbtgi_repository}
7	via the GAR REST API implemented by
8	{gar_service}.
9	Raw presence enumeration — no health classification (use {rbtgo_hallmark_tally} for that).
10	
11	The
12	{rbtr_director}
13	rekons over two domains, each backed by a distinct procedure:
14	
15	* `rbfl_rekon_hallmark` — basenames present under a hallmark subtree
16	* `rbfl_rekon_reliquary` — tool images present under a reliquary stamp's subtree
17	
18	Rekon over the legacy enshrinement domain is intentionally omitted: an enshrinement is a single image per anchor (1-deep), so no presence table is meaningful — {rbtgo_image_audit} covers the catalog and {rbtgo_image_retrieve} covers retrieval.
19	
20	== Hallmark Rekon
21	
22	The
23	{rbtr_director}
24	rekons basenames under a hallmark:
25	
26	//axhopt_typed_parameter
27	{rbst_hallmark}
28	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
29	.. {rbbc_require}
30	`«INPUT_HALLMARK»` provided as first argument
31	.. {rbbc_fatal}
32	if missing: "Usage: rbw-irh <hallmark>"
33	
34	//axhos_step
35	. *Authenticate as
36	{rbtr_director}*:
37	.. Execute
38	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
39	.. {rbbc_store}
40	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Enumerate Packages Under Hallmarks Root*:
44	.. {rbbc_store}
45	`«HALLMARKS_ROOT»` from `RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT` (kindled from `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS`, the token `rbi_hm`)
46	.. {rbbc_store}
47	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base (`projects/«PROJECT»/locations/«REGION»/repositories/«REPOSITORY»`)
48	.. {rbbc_call}
49	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
50	... method: `GET`
51	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
52	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
53	.. {rbbc_require}
54	HTTP 200 response
55	.. Extract `.packages[].name`, strip the resource prefix, URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`, and retain only entries beginning with `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/`
56	.. For each retained `<hallmark>/<basename>` package, {rbbc_call}
57	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages.tags/list[GAR REST API: tags.list] for the package and {rbbc_require} at least one tag returned (HTTP 200 with non-empty `.tags[]`). Skip packages with zero tags — these are post-jettison walking-dead awaiting the depot's cleanup policy (see {rbtgo_depot_levy} step *Create Container Repository*). Filtering at this read site decouples the display from GAR's lazy reclamation cadence.
58	.. {rbbc_store}
59	`«HALLMARK_ROWS»` as `«HALLMARK» «BASENAME»` pairs derived from the live-tagged entries (a 2-deep enumeration)
60	
61	//axhos_step
62	. *Filter to Input Hallmark*:
63	.. {rbbc_store}
64	`«PRESENT»` as the set of basenames in `«HALLMARK_ROWS»` whose hallmark equals `«INPUT_HALLMARK»`
65	.. {rbbc_fatal}
66	if `«PRESENT»` is empty: "Hallmark not found: «INPUT_HALLMARK»"
67	
68	//axhoot_typed_output
69	{rbtga_ark}
70	. *Display Results*:
71	.. {rbbc_show}
72	table header `BASENAME | EXISTS | PACKAGE-PATH`
73	.. For each canonical basename in the order
74	{rbtga_ark_image},
75	{rbtga_ark_about},
76	{rbtga_ark_vouch},
77	`attest`,
78	{rbtga_ark_pouch},
79	{rbtga_ark_diags}:
80	... {rbbc_store}
81	`«MARK»` as `yes` if `«BASENAME»` ∈ `«PRESENT»`, else `no`
82	... {rbbc_store}
83	`«PATH»` as `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/«INPUT_HALLMARK»/«BASENAME»` when `«MARK»` = `yes`, else the literal `(absent)`
84	... {rbbc_show}
85	row `«BASENAME» | «MARK» | «PATH»`
86	
87	== Reliquary Rekon
88	
89	The
90	{rbtr_director}
91	rekons tool images under a reliquary stamp:
92	
93	//axhopt_typed_parameter
94	{rbst_reliquary_stamp}
95	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
96	.. {rbbc_require}
97	`«INPUT_STAMP»` provided as first argument
98	.. {rbbc_fatal}
99	if missing: "Usage: rbw-irr <stamp>"
100	
101	//axhos_step
102	. *Authenticate as
103	{rbtr_director}*:
104	.. Execute
105	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
106	.. {rbbc_store}
107	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
108	
109	//axhos_step
110	. *Enumerate Packages Under Reliquaries Root*:
111	.. {rbbc_store}
112	`«RELIQUARIES_ROOT»` from `RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT` (kindled from `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES`, the token `rbi_rq`)
113	.. {rbbc_store}
114	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base (`projects/«PROJECT»/locations/«REGION»/repositories/«REPOSITORY»`)
115	.. {rbbc_call}
116	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
117	... method: `GET`
118	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
119	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
120	.. {rbbc_require}
121	HTTP 200 response
122	.. Extract `.packages[].name`, strip the resource prefix, URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`, and retain only entries beginning with `«RELIQUARIES_ROOT»/`
123	.. For each retained `<stamp>/<tool>` package, {rbbc_call}
124	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages.tags/list[GAR REST API: tags.list] for the package and {rbbc_require} at least one tag returned (HTTP 200 with non-empty `.tags[]`). Skip packages with zero tags — these are post-jettison walking-dead awaiting the depot's cleanup policy (see {rbtgo_depot_levy} step *Create Container Repository*). Filtering at this read site decouples the display from GAR's lazy reclamation cadence.
125	.. {rbbc_store}
126	`«RELIQUARY_ROWS»` as `«STAMP» «TOOL»` pairs derived from the live-tagged entries (a 2-deep enumeration)
127	
128	//axhos_step
129	. *Filter to Input Stamp*:
130	.. {rbbc_store}
131	`«PRESENT»` as the set of tools in `«RELIQUARY_ROWS»` whose stamp equals `«INPUT_STAMP»`
132	.. {rbbc_fatal}
133	if `«PRESENT»` is empty: "Reliquary stamp not found: «INPUT_STAMP»"
134	
135	//axhoot_typed_output
136	{rbst_reliquary_stamp}
137	. *Display Results*:
138	.. {rbbc_show}
139	table header `TOOL | EXISTS | PACKAGE-PATH`
140	.. For each canonical reliquary tool in the order `gcloud`, `docker`, `alpine`, `syft`, `binfmt`, `skopeo`:
141	... {rbbc_store}
142	`«MARK»` as `yes` if `«TOOL»` ∈ `«PRESENT»`, else `no`
143	... {rbbc_store}
144	`«PATH»` as `«RELIQUARIES_ROOT»/«INPUT_STAMP»/«TOOL»` when `«MARK»` = `yes`, else the literal `(absent)`
145	... {rbbc_show}
146	row `«TOOL» | «MARK» | «PATH»`
147	
148	//axhoc_completion
149	Each procedure completes successfully when the canonical row set has been displayed for the input identifier.
150	A subtree with no matching row across the canon is treated as the "not present" signal and reported as a fatal — empty subtrees do not yield empty tables.
151	The operation does not classify health; that is the role of {rbtgo_hallmark_tally}.
152

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_image_delete}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation jettisons a single image tag from
5	{rbtgi_repository}
6	using the Docker Registry HTTP API V2 implemented by
7	{gar_service}.
8	
9	`rbfl_jettison` is locator-generic: the same procedure backs
10	{rbtga_ark}
11	hallmark members,
12	reliquary tool images, and enshrinement images.
13	The package path embedded in the
14	{rbst_locator}
15	selects the domain — the procedure itself does not branch.
16	
17	NOTE: To delete an entire {rbtga_ark} (the full `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/` subtree — every basename including `image`, `about`, `vouch`, and any `pouch`/`diags`/`attest` tags present), use {rbtgo_ark_abjure} instead.
18	
19	NOTE: Jettisoning an interior member of a reliquary subtree (e.g., a single tool image) leaves a hole in an otherwise-coherent set. The operator accepts this piecemeal-by-image semantic; recovery is nuclear: re-run {rbtgo_depot_inscribe} to restore the full reliquary corpus. The operation provides no integrity guard against such interior holes.
20	
21	The
22	{rbtr_director}
23	jettisons an image by {rbst_locator}:
24	
25	//axhopt_typed_parameter
26	{rbst_locator}
27	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
28	.. {rbbc_require}
29	`«INPUT_LOCATOR»` provided as first argument in `package-path:tag` format
30	.. {rbbc_fatal}
31	if missing: "Locator parameter required (package-path:tag)"
32	.. {rbbc_fatal}
33	if malformed (no colon): "Invalid locator format. Expected package-path:tag"
34	.. {rbbc_store}
35	`«PACKAGE_PATH»` as portion before the final colon (e.g., `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image`, `{gar_reliquaries_namespace}/«STAMP»/syft`, `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»`)
36	.. {rbbc_store}
37	`«TAG»` as portion after the final colon
38	.. {rbbc_fatal}
39	if `«PACKAGE_PATH»` is empty: "Package path is empty in locator"
40	.. {rbbc_fatal}
41	if `«TAG»` is empty: "Tag is empty in locator"
42	
43	//axhos_step
44	. *Authenticate as
45	{rbtr_director}*:
46	.. Execute
47	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
48	.. {rbbc_store}
49	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
50	
51	//axhos_step
52	. *Confirm Jettison*:
53	.. {rbbc_show}
54	`Will jettison: «INPUT_LOCATOR»`
55	.. {rbbc_require}
56	user types `yes` to confirm
57	.. {rbbc_fatal}
58	if not confirmed: "prompt not confirmed."
59	
60	//axhos_step
61	. *Jettison Tag*:
62	.. {rbbc_call}
63	link:https://github.com/opencontainers/distribution-spec/blob/main/spec.md#deleting-tags[Docker Registry API: DELETE manifests] with:
64	... method: `DELETE`
65	... endpoint: `«REGISTRY_BASE»/«PACKAGE_PATH»/manifests/«TAG»`
66	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
67	.. {rbbc_require}
68	HTTP 202 (Accepted) or 204 (No Content) response
69	.. {rbbc_fatal}
70	if jettison fails: "Jettison failed with HTTP «STATUS_CODE»"
71	.. NOTE: V2 DELETE-by-tag has producer-side asymmetric semantics. For single-platform pushes (`docker push`), the tag points to a single image manifest; DELETE removes it, the package becomes empty, and GAR drops the package container from `ListPackages`. For multi-platform pushes (`docker buildx --push`, `skopeo copy --all`), the tag points to an OCI image index referencing N per-platform child manifests; DELETE removes only the index, leaving the N children as untagged orphan content. The package container persists in `ListPackages` because it still holds those untagged child manifests.
72	.. NOTE: The orphan-children residue is reclaimed autonomously by the depot's GAR cleanup policy — see {rbtgo_depot_levy} step *Create Container Repository* for the policy install, schema-verification sources, and cadence semantics. User-facing display surfaces ({rbtgo_hallmark_tally} and {rbtgo_image_rekon}) filter out packages with zero live tags, so tooling output reflects the correct post-jettison state immediately even though GAR-side reclamation is lazy. External observers querying GAR REST `ListPackages` directly will see the walking-dead package container until the cleanup policy's next run.
73	
74	//axhoot_typed_output
75	{rbst_locator}
76	. *Display Results*:
77	.. {rbbc_show}
78	`Jettisoned or nonexistent: «INPUT_LOCATOR»`
79	
80	//axhoc_completion
81	Procedure completes successfully when the tag has been jettisoned from the registry and the jettisoned message is displayed.
82	The locator-generic shape means a hallmark member, a reliquary tool image, or an enshrinement image are all deleted by the same call — domain identity lives in the locator's package path, not in branched logic.
83

content.subject: Repoint tool-resolution + preflights + yoke to conclave Lode sprued addressing
content.description: Move all reliquary tool addressing off legacy rbi_rq/<rel>/<tool>:<rel> onto conclave Lode rbi_ld/<rel>:rbi_<tool>. Sites: zrbfc_resolve_tool_images (rbfca), zrbfd_preflight_reliquary host curl (rbfd), rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh cloud step + the _RBGR_ subs feeding it (rbfd 746/795, 1406/1430; rbfv 337/378), rbfl_yoke GAR validation (rbfly). Compose RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE onto RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_* seeds. RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT -> RBGL_LODES_ROOT.
content.activeForm: Repointing tool resolution to conclave Lode addressing

content.subject: Build bole derived-pull ANCHOR populator
content.description: New consumption-side reader: after ensconce, read RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK + RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND (bole) and populate RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR in the vessel rbrv.env with the resolved bole Lode locator (rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_bole). Derived-pull, NOT yoke-stamp. Capture stays pure. Honors election rule: writes vessel config, operator-commits, never self-commits, does not self-gate on dirty tree.

content.subject: Finalize vessel-regime ANCHOR spec prose to wired derived-pull state
content.description: RBSRV, RBS0 rbrv_image_anchor/rbrv_image_origin, rbrv_regime.sh enrollment strings were left in transitional "populator pending" state by the enshrine-spec retirement. Finalize to wired derived-pull, dropping last enshrines-namespace residue in favor of the bole Lode rbi_ld locator.

content.subject: Update RBSDY to sprued member-tag addressing + derived cohort roster
content.description: RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc: update from legacy rbi_rq sub-package addressing to conclave Lode sprued :rbi_<tool> member-tag addressing, and the derived cohort roster.

content.subject: Retire RBSDI spec + rbtgo_depot_inscribe quoin
content.description: Retire RBSDI-depot_inscribe.adoc (live spec of retired operation) and rbtgo_depot_inscribe quoin in RBS0-SpecTop.adoc (mapping attribute + operation section + include). No cross-ref dangles. Land before inscribe-code retirement (both touch RBS0 mapping section per paddock side-lane note).

content.subject: Retire inscribe code path
content.description: Retire rbfli_Inscribe.sh, rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh cloud step, rbw-dI DirectorInscribesReliquary tabtarget, and the RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY zipper colophon. Update inscribe-tabtarget hints in rbfly/rbfd recovery prose. rbrd_inscribe (rbw-rdi) is OUT of scope — leave it.

content.subject: Retire four rbi_rq maintenance verbs + backing
content.description: Retire rbw-iar (AuditsReliquaries), rbw-irr (RekonsReliquary), rbw-iwr (WrestsReliquaryImage), rbw-iJr (JettisonsReliquaryImage) tabtargets + their backing paths in rbfln_Inventory/rbflw_Wrest/rbfcg_GarRest + zipper colophons. Path-polymorphic image backdoor subsumes them. Update RBSIA/RBSIR spec refs to rbi_rq.

content.subject: Flip onboarding inscribe/reliquary tracks
content.description: RBYC_RELIQUARY linked term, the literal inscribe prose in handbook tracks (rbhodf/rbhodb/rbhodg etc.), and the matching rbtdro onboarding fixture. Prose is not test-guarded; the done is the only guard.

content.subject: Build theurge + shellcheck + fast tests
content.description: Run tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh (regenerates rbtdgc_consts.rs + claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md from zipper), tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh, and tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh. Resolve any breakage before the live verify gate.
content.activeForm: Building theurge and running fast checks

content.subject: Operator-gated: live conjure-green verify + banish rbi_rq last
content.description: Verify gate is one conjure build green (needs Director credentials + cloud cost + a fresh conclave Lode yoked). Then banish rbi_rq namespace LAST. Both need operator: live GCP, cost, and a re-conclave+re-yoke of the cohort onto rbi_ld. Checkpoint with operator before running.
content.activeForm: Awaiting operator for live verify and rbi_rq banish

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Common Constants - File paths and naming conventions

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBCC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBCC_SOURCED=1

# Kit directory — source-time self-location. rbcc lives at the RBK kit root,
# so its own directory IS the kit dir. No launcher environment dependency;
# available the instant this file is sourced (BCG kit-self-location pattern,
# canon: rbtd/rbte_cli.sh).
readonly RBCC_KIT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

# Generated zipper-derived artifacts — the theurge build materializes both
# (write-on-change), rbq's gates verify them. Absolute, composed from the
# readonly kit dir (source-time, no kindle dependency).
readonly RBCC_rbtdgc_consts_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/rbtd/src/rbtdgc_consts.rs"
readonly RBCC_tabtarget_context_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md"

# ── Moorings inventory constants ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Moorings-relative path values. Every consumer reads these names directly
# (the transitional RBBC_* aliases were retired by the literal-sweep pace).
# Source-time literals, no kindle dependency.
RBCC_moorings_dir="rbmm_moorings"
RBCC_launchers_subdir="rbml_launchers"
RBCC_users_subdir="rbmu_users"
RBCC_nodes_subdir="rbmn_nodes"
RBCC_vessels_subdir="rbmv_vessels"
RBCC_rbrr_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrr.env"
RBCC_rbrp_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrp.env"
RBCC_rbrm_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrm.env"
RBCC_rbrd_basename="rbrd.env"
RBCC_rbrd_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBCC_rbrd_basename}"

# Literal constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
RBCC_rbrs_file="../station-files/rbrs.env"
RBCC_rbrn_file="rbrn.env"
RBCC_rbra_file="rbra.env"
RBCC_rbro_file="rbro.env"
# Auth-role enum (rbnae_): the RBRA_ROLE value — minted sprue, RBRA_ROLE domain
# (3 members). Written into credential files, validated, carried as the auth
# folio, swizzle-checked against BUZ_FOLIO. Distinct axis from RBCC_account_*
# below: that one is the bare composition label.
RBCC_role_governor="rbnae_governor"
RBCC_role_retriever="rbnae_retriever"
RBCC_role_director="rbnae_director"

# Account composition labels — bare fragments that compose GCP SA account-ids/
# emails AND local secret-directory names (6 members, superset of the 3 roles
# plus payor/assay/mason which are NEVER RBRA_ROLE values). These stay bare:
# a derived resource-name string in cloud/filesystem space, structurally like
# the SA email and the -tether/-airgap pool suffixes the heat keeps bare.
RBCC_account_governor="governor"
RBCC_account_retriever="retriever"
RBCC_account_director="director"
RBCC_account_payor="payor"
RBCC_account_assay="assay"
RBCC_account_mason="mason"
RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"

# Operation-verb tinder — the canonical bash home for RBK operation verbs.
# Members are bare verb tokens; the group carries one author here so it is
# projectable under the single-canonical-author rule. Two surfaces:
#
#   SA-management (divest/invest/roster) — composed into the fact-extension
#   constants below; consumed by the governor/director account surface.
RBCC_verb_divest="divest"
RBCC_verb_invest="invest"
RBCC_verb_roster="roster"
#
#   Image/build lifecycle (inscribe/kludge/ordain/yoke) — name the
#   registry and build operations. Previously implicit in command-function
#   names (rbfl_inscribe, rbfd_ordain, …) and tabtarget descriptions; homed
#   here so the group has a single owner rather than being reconstructed by
#   grep across rbfd_/rbfl_/rbfk_/rbob_.
RBCC_verb_inscribe="inscribe"
RBCC_verb_kludge="kludge"
RBCC_verb_ordain="ordain"
RBCC_verb_yoke="yoke"

# Fact-file extension tinder — multi-fact registry for buf_write_fact_multi.
# Producers emit "<basename>.<extension>" via filesystem-as-data-bus pattern;
# consumers walk fact files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR keyed on extension.
# Roster extensions composed from earlier tinder (BCG tinder-on-tinder).
RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_retriever}"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_director}"
RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark="audit-hallmark"

# Container-role tinder — the canonical bash home for the crucible's container
# roles. Bare role tokens; the crucible is sentry + pentacle + bottle and every
# container name / compose service derives from these. Distinct from the
# credential auth-role axis above, which itself splits in two: RBCC_role_*
# (minted enum, the RBRA_ROLE value) and RBCC_account_* (bare composition label
# for SA names + secret dirs). None of these words are reused across families,
# keeping each token monosemous.
RBCC_container_bottle="bottle"
RBCC_container_pentacle="pentacle"
RBCC_container_sentry="sentry"

######################################################################
# Rust const projection (rbcc single-homed set → RBTDGC_)

# rbcc_emit_consts() - Emit the RBCC-owned co-maintained constants as Rust
# string consts to stdout, one `pub const` line per name/value pair via the
# shared buz_emit_const primitive (BUK must be kindled). The single-homed set:
# moorings/vessels dirs, account labels, .env filenames, operation verbs, and
# container roles. Each Rust const is
# RBTDGC_ + the RBCC stem (RBCC_ prefix stripped) uppercased; the value is
# carried verbatim. Bash stays mixed-case (RBCC_moorings_dir); the generated
# Rust is SCREAMING (RBTDGC_MOORINGS_DIR) per Rust convention — that casing is
# the sole transform, applied mechanically, so there is no per-entry mapping
# and no drift. rbtd's lib.rs paths, the manifest account-label mirror, and the
# rbtdrk/rbtdrp .env consts all source these instead of hand-copying.
rbcc_emit_consts() {
  printf '%s\n' "// RBCC constants (rbcc_Constants.sh single-homed set)"

  local z_name=""
  local z_stem=""
  local z_upper=""
  for z_name in \
    RBCC_moorings_dir    \
    RBCC_vessels_subdir  \
    RBCC_account_governor   \
    RBCC_account_retriever  \
    RBCC_account_director   \
    RBCC_account_payor      \
    RBCC_account_assay      \
    RBCC_account_mason      \
    RBCC_rbrr_file       \
    RBCC_rbrp_file       \
    RBCC_rbrm_file       \
    RBCC_rbrd_basename   \
    RBCC_rbrd_file       \
    RBCC_rbrs_file       \
    RBCC_rbrn_file       \
    RBCC_rbra_file       \
    RBCC_rbro_file       \
    RBCC_verb_divest     \
    RBCC_verb_invest     \
    RBCC_verb_roster     \
    RBCC_verb_inscribe   \
    RBCC_verb_kludge     \
    RBCC_verb_ordain     \
    RBCC_verb_yoke       \
    RBCC_container_bottle    \
    RBCC_container_pentacle  \
    RBCC_container_sentry    \
  ; do
    z_stem="${z_name#RBCC_}"
    z_upper="$(printf '%s' "${z_stem}" | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')"
    buz_emit_const "RBTDGC_${z_upper}" "${!z_name}" \
      || buc_die "rbcc_emit_consts: emit failed for ${z_name}"
  done
}

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbcc_*)

zrbcc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc already kindled"

  # Curl timeout bounds — all actionable curl sites use these
  readonly RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC=10
  readonly RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC=60

  readonly ZRBCC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbcc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbcc not kindled - call zrbcc_kindle first"
}

# eof

+#   names (rbrd_inscribe, rbfd_ordain, …) and tabtarget descriptions; homed
#   here so the group has a single owner rather than being reconstructed by

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Lode - reliquary body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
#   conclave — convene the build-tool cohort into a Lode (Director credentials)
# The reliquary rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
# kind-specific data — the conclave recipe (gcrane cohort capture + vouch-push),
# the substitutions blob, and the touchmark-fact extract — and composes
# them through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
# step-composition machinery lives here.
#
# Conclave absorbs today's inscribe pull machinery (rbfli_Inscribe + rbgji01),
# retargeted from the rbi_rq/<date>/<tool> sibling-package layout to one rbi_ld
# package holding N member tags (:rbi_<tool>) plus the :rbi_vouch envelope. Both
# steps ride the floating gcrane builder (ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER): the tools captured
# here ARE the reliquary, so capture cannot bootstrap from one — conclave is the
# generation phase the pinning rule permits to run unpinned (RBS0 rbsk_pinning_boundary).

set -euo pipefail

# Conclave is capture-pure: it writes no consumer config. It hands the captured
# touchmark to a later explicit yoke-stamp election (the reliquary cutover, a
# separate pace) through two bare single-form chaining facts
# (RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK + RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND/RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY). The
# provenance envelope lives only in GAR (:rbi_vouch tag, pushed cloud-side by
# rbgjl02), never host-side.

######################################################################
# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)

# Internal: compose the conclave capture recipe (gcrane cohort capture + vouch-push)
# and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit
# and poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
# inscribe-borrowed poll ceiling (the cohort copy needs the larger ceiling — same
# work inscribe performs today).
# Args: token stamp
zrbld_conclave_submit() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
  local -r z_stamp="${2:?Stamp required}"

  buc_step "Constructing conclave capture recipe"
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
  # spine. Both steps ride the floating gcrane builder (busybox entrypoint —
  # gcrane:debug's only shell) — no reliquary bootstrap (conclave IS what captures
  # the reliquary tools; generation-tier, the one phase allowed unpinned).
  local -r z_recipe=(
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl03-conclave-capture.sh|${ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER}|conclave-capture|busybox"
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER}|assemble-push-vouch|busybox"
  )

  buc_log_args "Composing conclave substitutions blob"
  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}subs.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_sprue    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
    --arg zjq_stamp        "${z_stamp}" \
    '{
      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:     $zjq_gar_host,
      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:     $zjq_gar_path,
      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:   $zjq_lodes_root,
      _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE:    $zjq_tag_sprue,
      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:    $zjq_tag_vouch,
      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:  $zjq_trust_grade,
      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA: $zjq_vouch_schema,
      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:  $zjq_acquired_by,
      _RBGL_LODE_STAMP:   $zjq_stamp
    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose conclave substitutions blob"

  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
    "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "Conclave" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_INSCRIBE}" \
    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}" \
    "${z_recipe[@]}"
}

# Internal: extract the captured touchmark from the completed conclave build and
# emit the two bare single-form chaining facts (touchmark value + kind-brand
# enum). The capture step (step 0) authors the base64 JSON carrying the
# host-minted stamp in slot_1; the vouch-push step writes no output. Conclave
# captures exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package), so exactly one slot is
# populated. The provenance envelope is NOT read host-side: it lives only in GAR
# (rbgjl02 pushed it under :rbi_vouch), so the host hands forward only the
# touchmark a consumer needs.
zrbld_conclave_extract() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"

  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}output.json"
  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"

  buc_log_args "Conclave output:"
  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"

  local -r z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}stamp.txt"
  jq -r '.rbls_slot_1.rbls_stamp // empty' "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to read reliquary stamp from conclave output"
  local -r z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
  test -n "${z_stamp}" || buc_die "Conclave output carried no stamp in rbls_slot_1"

  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" "${z_stamp}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write touchmark fact for ${z_stamp}"
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}" "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write kind-brand fact for ${z_stamp}"
  buc_success "Conclave captured Lode ${z_stamp} — touchmark fact emitted (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY})"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbld_*)

rbld_conclave() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Convene the build-tool cohort into one Lode (reliquary kind, rbi_ld capture)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
  # The reliquary kind-letter 'r' matches the legacy inscribe datestamp prefix,
  # but the namespaces differ (rbi_ld vs rbi_rq), so no collision.
  local -r z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"

  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"

  zrbld_conclave_submit "${z_token}" "${z_stamp}"
  zrbld_conclave_extract

  buc_success "Conclave complete: build-tool cohort -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
}

# eof

# GCB step execution; mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim (the
# two coexist during the reliquary cutover). gcloud, docker, and gcrane are
# Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane rides the :debug variant
# so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell its capture steps need.
# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
# so the cohort cannot be expressed as the `MANIFEST=( … )` array rbgji01 (running
# under bash) still uses; a while-read over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form,
# matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.

# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — authoritative co-versioned set for
# GCB step execution; mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim (the
# two coexist during the reliquary cutover). gcloud, docker, and gcrane are
# Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane rides the :debug variant
# so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell its capture steps need.

toolUseResult.newString:
# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — the authoritative co-versioned set
# for GCB step execution (the legacy inscribe manifest rbgji01 is retired). gcloud,
# docker, and gcrane are Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane
# rides the :debug variant so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell
# its capture steps need.
# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
# so the cohort cannot be expressed as a bash `MANIFEST=( … )` array; a while-read
# over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form, matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 03: Conclave the build-tool cohort into a Lode (capture) via gcrane
# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (Google-hosted, always pullable —
#          conclave captures the reliquary tools themselves, so it cannot bootstrap
#          from a reliquary; gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently via its google.Keychain
#          -> ADC -> the GCE metadata server as the Mason SA, no explicit login. The
#          :debug variant carries /busybox/sh for the orchestration. Auth canon: RBSCB)
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
#                _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY
#
# Pull each build-tool image from upstream, tag it into ONE GAR package
# rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean member tag :rbi_<tool>, and push. Author the
# batch provenance envelope (members[] one per tool — the cardinality axis) and
# stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
# /builder/outputs/output. Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB
# steps on amd64 workers; gcrane cp --platform linux/amd64 copies just the amd64
# manifest registry->registry (daemonless — no docker daemon, no pull/tag/push).
#
# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
# Member tags on that package, each a distinct tool manifest:
#   :<TAG_SPRUE><tool>   e.g. rbi_gcloud, rbi_skopeo   (clean scheme — no digest layer)
# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Conclave build-tool cohort into a Lode ==="

STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }

PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"

# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — authoritative co-versioned set for
# GCB step execution; mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim (the
# two coexist during the reliquary cutover). gcloud, docker, and gcrane are
# Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane rides the :debug variant
# so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell its capture steps need.
# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
# so the cohort cannot be expressed as the `MANIFEST=( … )` array rbgji01 (running
# under bash) still uses; a while-read over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form,
# matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.

# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole cohort.
ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

# Accumulate the envelope members[] as we capture (one element per tool). No jq
# dependency — values are controlled (tool name, upstream ref, hex digest, SA
# email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
MEMBERS=''
MFIRST=true

while IFS='|' read -r NAME UPSTREAM; do
  test -n "${NAME}" || continue
  MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_SPRUE}${NAME}"
  DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"

  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST} ---"

  # gcrane cp copies registry->registry by digest, daemonless (no docker pull/tag/push).
  # --platform linux/amd64 flattens a multi-arch upstream to the SINGLE amd64 manifest.
  # This is conclave-ONLY and must not leak to other Lode kinds (bole/wsl keep full-
  # fidelity capture): the reliquary cohort is consumed solely as GCB step images on
  # amd64 workers, so the other platforms are dead bytes — and a full multi-arch index
  # makes the Lode package a parent-index/child-manifest web, where GAR refuses to
  # delete a child while its parent index exists (FAILED_PRECONDITION, "referenced by
  # parent manifests") and a single packages.delete removes nothing — banish then
  # needs multiple convergence rounds instead of one. References: RBSLC, RBSLB, RBSCB.
  gcrane --platform linux/amd64 cp "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Record the upstream manifest-list digest for the envelope. gcrane digest (no
  # --platform) streams the tag's stored index digest (sha256:...) — the same canonical
  # value docker's RepoDigests reported, unchanged by the single-platform copy above, so
  # recorded digests stay identical to the pre-eviction docker path. CBb_101 applies.
  DIGEST=$(gcrane digest "${UPSTREAM}") \
    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane digest failed for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
  test -n "${DIGEST}" || { echo "FATAL: empty digest for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "${NAME} captured: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"

  if [ "${MFIRST}" = "true" ]; then MFIRST=false; else MEMBERS="${MEMBERS},"; fi
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}{"
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"rblv_name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"rblv_origin\":\"${UPSTREAM}\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"rblv_digest\":\"${DIGEST}\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"rblv_verification\":\"oci-digest\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"rblv_tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}}"
done <<'MANIFEST'
gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest
docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest
alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest
syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest
binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest
skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest
gcrane|gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug
MANIFEST

# Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and
# the host capture-file). rblv_members[] is the cardinality axis — N for the reliquary
# cohort, where bole carries 1.
ENVELOPE='{'
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_kind\":\"reliquary\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_signature\":null,"
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_members\":[${MEMBERS}]}"

# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
# stamps file is the step-02 contract; conclave produces exactly one Lode.
printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
# conclave produces exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package).
RESULT="{\"rbls_slot_1\":{\"rbls_stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"rbls_vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"

echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts the touchmark -> capture-file).
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Conclave capture step complete ==="

input.old_string: NOTE: Jettisoning an interior member of a reliquary subtree (e.g., a single tool image) leaves a hole in an otherwise-coherent set. The operator accepts this piecemeal-by-image semantic; recovery is nuclear: re-run {rbtgo_depot_inscribe} to restore the full reliquary corpus. The operation provides no integrity guard against such interior holes.
input.new_string: NOTE: Jettisoning an interior member of a reliquary Lode (e.g., a single tool image member tag) leaves a hole in an otherwise-coherent cohort. The operator accepts this piecemeal-by-image semantic; recovery is nuclear: re-run {rbtgo_lode_conclave} to restore the full reliquary corpus. The operation provides no integrity guard against such interior holes.

input.old_string:
NOTE: This operation reports which canonical members are present under a
hallmark or reliquary subtree in
{rbtgi_repository}
via the GAR REST API implemented by
{gar_service}.
Raw presence enumeration — no health classification (use {rbtgo_hallmark_tally} for that).

The
{rbtr_director}
rekons over two domains, each backed by a distinct procedure:

* `rbfl_rekon_hallmark` — basenames present under a hallmark subtree
* `rbfl_rekon_reliquary` — tool images present under a reliquary stamp's subtree

Rekon over the legacy enshrinement domain is intentionally omitted: an enshrinement is a single image per anchor (1-deep), so no presence table is meaningful — {rbtgo_image_audit} covers the catalog and {rbtgo_image_retrieve} covers retrieval.

content.content:
83	... {rbbc_show}
84	row `«BASENAME» | «MARK» | «PATH»`
85	
86	== Reliquary Rekon
87	
88	The
89	{rbtr_director}
90	rekons tool images under a reliquary stamp:
91	
92	//axhopt_typed_parameter
93	{rbst_reliquary_stamp}
94	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
95	.. {rbbc_require}
96	`«INPUT_STAMP»` provided as first argument
97	.. {rbbc_fatal}
98	if missing: "Usage: rbw-irr <stamp>"
99	
100	//axhos_step
101	. *Authenticate as
102	{rbtr_director}*:
103	.. Execute
104	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
105	.. {rbbc_store}
106	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
107	
108	//axhos_step
109	. *Enumerate Packages Under Reliquaries Root*:
110	.. {rbbc_store}
111	`«RELIQUARIES_ROOT»` from `RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT` (kindled from `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES`, the token `rbi_rq`)
112	.. {rbbc_store}
113	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base (`projects/«PROJECT»/locations/«REGION»/repositories/«REPOSITORY»`)
114	.. {rbbc_call}
115	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
116	... method: `GET`
117	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
118	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
119	.. {rbbc_require}
120	HTTP 200 response
121	.. Extract `.packages[].name`, strip the resource prefix, URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`, and retain only entries beginning with `«RELIQUARIES_ROOT»/`
122	.. For each retained `<stamp>/<tool>` package, {rbbc_call}
123	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages.tags/list[GAR REST API: tags.list] for the package and {rbbc_require} at least one tag returned (HTTP 200 with non-empty `.tags[]`). Skip packages with zero tags — these are post-jettison walking-dead awaiting the depot's cleanup policy (see {rbtgo_depot_levy} step *Create Container Repository*). Filtering at this read site decouples the display from GAR's lazy reclamation cadence.
124	.. {rbbc_store}
125	`«RELIQUARY_ROWS»` as `«STAMP» «TOOL»` pairs derived from the live-tagged entries (a 2-deep enumeration)
126	
127	//axhos_step
128	. *Filter to Input Stamp*:
129	.. {rbbc_store}
130	`«PRESENT»` as the set of tools in `«RELIQUARY_ROWS»` whose stamp equals `«INPUT_STAMP»`
131	.. {rbbc_fatal}
132	if `«PRESENT»` is empty: "Reliquary stamp not found: «INPUT_STAMP»"
133	
134	//axhoot_typed_output
135	{rbst_reliquary_stamp}
136	. *Display Results*:
137	.. {rbbc_show}
138	table header `TOOL | EXISTS | PACKAGE-PATH`
139	.. For each canonical reliquary tool in the order `gcloud`, `docker`, `alpine`, `syft`, `binfmt`, `skopeo`:
140	... {rbbc_store}
141	`«MARK»` as `yes` if `«TOOL»` ∈ `«PRESENT»`, else `no`
142	... {rbbc_store}
143	`«PATH»` as `«RELIQUARIES_ROOT»/«INPUT_STAMP»/«TOOL»` when `«MARK»` = `yes`, else the literal `(absent)`
144	... {rbbc_show}
145	row `«TOOL» | «MARK» | «PATH»`
146	
147	//axhoc_completion
148	Each procedure completes successfully when the canonical row set has been displayed for the input identifier.
149	A subtree with no matching row across the canon is treated as the "not present" signal and reported as a fatal — empty subtrees do not yield empty tables.
150	The operation does not classify health; that is the role of {rbtgo_hallmark_tally}.

content.subject: Repoint tool-resolution + preflights + yoke to conclave Lode sprued addressing
content.description: Move all reliquary tool addressing off legacy rbi_rq/<rel>/<tool>:<rel> onto conclave Lode rbi_ld/<rel>:rbi_<tool>. Sites: zrbfc_resolve_tool_images (rbfca), zrbfd_preflight_reliquary host curl (rbfd), rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh cloud step + the _RBGR_ subs feeding it (rbfd 746/795, 1406/1430; rbfv 337/378), rbfl_yoke GAR validation (rbfly). Compose RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE onto RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_* seeds. RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT -> RBGL_LODES_ROOT.
content.activeForm: Repointing tool resolution to conclave Lode addressing

content.subject: Build bole derived-pull ANCHOR populator
content.description: New consumption-side reader: after ensconce, read RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK + RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND (bole) and populate RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR in the vessel rbrv.env with the resolved bole Lode locator (rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_bole). Derived-pull, NOT yoke-stamp. Capture stays pure. Honors election rule: writes vessel config, operator-commits, never self-commits, does not self-gate on dirty tree.

content.subject: Finalize vessel-regime ANCHOR spec prose to wired derived-pull state
content.description: RBSRV, RBS0 rbrv_image_anchor/rbrv_image_origin, rbrv_regime.sh enrollment strings were left in transitional "populator pending" state by the enshrine-spec retirement. Finalize to wired derived-pull, dropping last enshrines-namespace residue in favor of the bole Lode rbi_ld locator.

content.subject: Update RBSDY to sprued member-tag addressing + derived cohort roster
content.description: RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc: update from legacy rbi_rq sub-package addressing to conclave Lode sprued :rbi_<tool> member-tag addressing, and the derived cohort roster.

content.subject: Retire RBSDI spec + rbtgo_depot_inscribe quoin
content.description: Retire RBSDI-depot_inscribe.adoc (live spec of retired operation) and rbtgo_depot_inscribe quoin in RBS0-SpecTop.adoc (mapping attribute + operation section + include). No cross-ref dangles. Land before inscribe-code retirement (both touch RBS0 mapping section per paddock side-lane note).

content.subject: Retire inscribe code path
content.description: Retire rbfli_Inscribe.sh, rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh cloud step, rbw-dI DirectorInscribesReliquary tabtarget, and the RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY zipper colophon. Update inscribe-tabtarget hints in rbfly/rbfd recovery prose. rbrd_inscribe (rbw-rdi) is OUT of scope — leave it.

content.subject: Retire four rbi_rq maintenance verbs + backing
content.description: Retire rbw-iar (AuditsReliquaries), rbw-irr (RekonsReliquary), rbw-iwr (WrestsReliquaryImage), rbw-iJr (JettisonsReliquaryImage) tabtargets + their backing paths in rbfln_Inventory/rbflw_Wrest/rbfcg_GarRest + zipper colophons. Path-polymorphic image backdoor subsumes them. Update RBSIA/RBSIR spec refs to rbi_rq.

content.subject: Flip onboarding inscribe/reliquary tracks
content.description: RBYC_RELIQUARY linked term, the literal inscribe prose in handbook tracks (rbhodf/rbhodb/rbhodg etc.), and the matching rbtdro onboarding fixture. Prose is not test-guarded; the done is the only guard.

content.subject: Build theurge + shellcheck + fast tests
content.description: Run tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh (regenerates rbtdgc_consts.rs + claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md from zipper), tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh, and tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh. Resolve any breakage before the live verify gate.
content.activeForm: Building theurge and running fast checks

content.subject: Operator-gated: live conjure-green verify + banish rbi_rq last
content.description: Verify gate is one conjure build green (needs Director credentials + cloud cost + a fresh conclave Lode yoked). Then banish rbi_rq namespace LAST. Both need operator: live GCP, cost, and a re-conclave+re-yoke of the cohort onto rbi_ld. Checkpoint with operator before running.
content.activeForm: Awaiting operator for live verify and rbi_rq banish

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_image_audit}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation produces a flat catalog of identifiers under one of the three GAR domains
5	(hallmarks, reliquaries, enshrinements)
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	via the GAR REST API implemented by
9	{gar_service}.
10	Identifiers only — no per-member presence detail (use {rbtgo_image_rekon} for that), and no health classification (use {rbtgo_hallmark_tally} for that).
11	
12	The
13	{rbtr_director}
14	audits over three domains, each backed by a distinct procedure:
15	
16	* `rbfl_audit_hallmarks` — every hallmark identifier under the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/` root
17	* `rbfl_audit_reliquaries` — every reliquary stamp under the `{gar_reliquaries_namespace}/` root
18	* `rbfl_audit_enshrinements` — every enshrined base anchor under the `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` root
19	
20	Audit vs. {rbtgo_hallmark_tally}: audit is a {rbtr_director}-tier flat-list operation across all three domains; tally is a {rbtr_retriever}-tier hallmarks-only operation that classifies each entry by basename completeness and vouch status.
21	The two roles compose without overlap.
22

24

26	{rbtr_director}
27	audits hallmark identifiers:

Sites: zrbfc_resolve_tool_images (rbfca), zrbfd_preflight_reliquary host curl (rbfd), rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh cloud step + the _RBGR_ subs feeding it (rbfd 746/795, 1406/1430; rbfv 337/378), rbfl_yoke GAR validation (rbfly). Compose RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE onto RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_* seeds. RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT -> RBGL_LODES_ROOT.

content.subject: Build bole derived-pull ANCHOR populator
content.description: New consumption-side reader: after ensconce, read RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK + RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND (bole) and populate RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR in the vessel rbrv.env with the resolved bole Lode locator (rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_bole). Derived-pull, NOT yoke-stamp. Capture stays pure. Honors election rule: writes vessel config, operator-commits, never self-commits, does not self-gate on dirty tree.

content.subject: Finalize vessel-regime ANCHOR spec prose to wired derived-pull state
content.description: RBSRV, RBS0 rbrv_image_anchor/rbrv_image_origin, rbrv_regime.sh enrollment strings were left in transitional "populator pending" state by the enshrine-spec retirement. Finalize to wired derived-pull, dropping last enshrines-namespace residue in favor of the bole Lode rbi_ld locator.

content.subject: Update RBSDY to sprued member-tag addressing + derived cohort roster
content.description: RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc: update from legacy rbi_rq sub-package addressing to conclave Lode sprued :rbi_<tool> member-tag addressing, and the derived cohort roster.

No cross-ref dangles. Land before inscribe-code retirement (both touch RBS0 mapping section per paddock side-lane note).

content.subject: Retire inscribe code path
content.description: Retire rbfli_Inscribe.sh, rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh cloud step, rbw-dI DirectorInscribesReliquary tabtarget, and the RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY zipper colophon. Update inscribe-tabtarget hints in rbfly/rbfd recovery prose. rbrd_inscribe (rbw-rdi) is OUT of scope — leave it.

content.subject: Retire four rbi_rq maintenance verbs + backing
content.description: Retire rbw-iar (AuditsReliquaries), rbw-irr (RekonsReliquary), rbw-iwr (WrestsReliquaryImage), rbw-iJr (JettisonsReliquaryImage) tabtargets + their backing paths in rbfln_Inventory/rbflw_Wrest/rbfcg_GarRest + zipper colophons. Path-polymorphic image backdoor subsumes them. Update RBSIA/RBSIR spec refs to rbi_rq.

content.description: RBYC_RELIQUARY linked term, the literal inscribe prose in handbook tracks (rbhodf/rbhodb/rbhodg etc.), and the matching rbtdro onboarding fixture. Prose is not test-guarded; the done is the only guard.

content.subject: Repoint tool-resolution + preflights + yoke to conclave Lode sprued addressing
content.description: Move all reliquary tool addressing off legacy rbi_rq/<rel>/<tool>:<rel> onto conclave Lode rbi_ld/<rel>:rbi_<tool>. Sites: zrbfc_resolve_tool_images (rbfca), zrbfd_preflight_reliquary host curl (rbfd), rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh cloud step + the _RBGR_ subs feeding it (rbfd 746/795, 1406/1430; rbfv 337/378), rbfl_yoke GAR validation (rbfly). Compose RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE onto RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_* seeds. RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT -> RBGL_LODES_ROOT.
content.activeForm: Repointing tool resolution to conclave Lode addressing

content.subject: Build bole derived-pull ANCHOR populator
content.description: New consumption-side reader: after ensconce, read RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK + RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND (bole) and populate RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR in the vessel rbrv.env with the resolved bole Lode locator (rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_bole). Derived-pull, NOT yoke-stamp. Capture stays pure. Honors election rule: writes vessel config, operator-commits, never self-commits, does not self-gate on dirty tree.

content.subject: Finalize vessel-regime ANCHOR spec prose to wired derived-pull state
content.description: RBSRV, RBS0 rbrv_image_anchor/rbrv_image_origin, rbrv_regime.sh enrollment strings were left in transitional "populator pending" state by the enshrine-spec retirement. Finalize to wired derived-pull, dropping last enshrines-namespace residue in favor of the bole Lode rbi_ld locator.

content.subject: Update RBSDY to sprued member-tag addressing + derived cohort roster
content.description: RBSDY-director_yoke.adoc: update from legacy rbi_rq sub-package addressing to conclave Lode sprued :rbi_<tool> member-tag addressing, and the derived cohort roster.

content.subject: Retire RBSDI spec + rbtgo_depot_inscribe quoin
content.description: Retire RBSDI-depot_inscribe.adoc (live spec of retired operation) and rbtgo_depot_inscribe quoin in RBS0-SpecTop.adoc (mapping attribute + operation section + include). No cross-ref dangles. Land before inscribe-code retirement (both touch RBS0 mapping section per paddock side-lane note).

content.subject: Retire inscribe code path
content.description: Retire rbfli_Inscribe.sh, rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh cloud step, rbw-dI DirectorInscribesReliquary tabtarget, and the RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY zipper colophon. Update inscribe-tabtarget hints in rbfly/rbfd recovery prose. rbrd_inscribe (rbw-rdi) is OUT of scope — leave it.

content.subject: Retire four rbi_rq maintenance verbs + backing
content.description: Retire rbw-iar (AuditsReliquaries), rbw-irr (RekonsReliquary), rbw-iwr (WrestsReliquaryImage), rbw-iJr (JettisonsReliquaryImage) tabtargets + their backing paths in rbfln_Inventory/rbflw_Wrest/rbfcg_GarRest + zipper colophons. Path-polymorphic image backdoor subsumes them. Update RBSIA/RBSIR spec refs to rbi_rq.

content.subject: Flip onboarding inscribe/reliquary tracks
content.description: RBYC_RELIQUARY linked term, the literal inscribe prose in handbook tracks (rbhodf/rbhodb/rbhodg etc.), and the matching rbtdro onboarding fixture. Prose is not test-guarded; the done is the only guard.

content.subject: Build theurge + shellcheck + fast tests
content.description: Run tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh (regenerates rbtdgc_consts.rs + claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md from zipper), tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh, and tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh. Resolve any breakage before the live verify gate.
content.activeForm: Building theurge and running fast checks

content.subject: Operator-gated: live conjure-green verify + banish rbi_rq last
content.description: Verify gate is one conjure build green (needs Director credentials + cloud cost + a fresh conclave Lode yoked). Then banish rbi_rq namespace LAST. Both need operator: live GCP, cost, and a re-conclave+re-yoke of the cohort onto rbi_ld. Checkpoint with operator before running.
content.activeForm: Awaiting operator for live verify and rbi_rq banish

content.text:
`₢BHAAM` is wrapped (state-only transition — the code was already committed at `a510073d4`; nothing was lost). Pencils down. Here's the handoff.

---

# Statement of Concern — `₣BH` cutover handoff (`₢BHAAM` wrapped early)

**Prepared at session end. The reliquary inscribe→conclave cutover is committed (`a510073d4`) and statically green, but `₢BHAAM` was wrapped at operator direction with two of its own `Done when` criteria unmet. Read this before relying on the conclave path or mounting the next pace.**

## HIGH — the central repoint is committed but *never exercised end-to-end*

The load-bearing change — tool resolution moving from `rbi_rq/<rel>/<tool>:<rel>` to the conclave Lode `rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_<tool>` — is verified **only by static analysis** (shellcheck, an independent BCG/CBG review, and my reading that the resolver's tag scheme matches what `rbgjl03` writes). **The fast suite does not touch the build path** — it's regime/enrollment/handbook tests. So nothing has yet proven that conjure actually pulls the right tool images from `rbi_ld`. The docket's `Verify gate: one conjure build green` is the *first real exercise* of this change, and it was not run (no GCP creds/cost in this session).

## HIGH — operational gotcha: the live system is in a half-cut state

Every vessel's `rbrv.env` still holds `RBRV_RELIQUARY=r260609093011`, which is an **`rbi_rq` touchmark**. The new resolver now looks for that touchmark in **`rbi_ld`**, where it does not exist. **A conjure/ordain right now would fail.** Before any build works, the operator must: `conclave` (mint a fresh `rbi_ld` Lode → new touchmark) → `yoke` that touchmark across vessels → `ordain` a conjure vessel and confirm green. This is the deferred `#10` and the verify gate in one sequence.

## HIGH — `₢BHAAM` closed with unmet `Done when` criteria

Do not read the wrap as "docket fully satisfied." Unmet at close: **`conjure green`** and **`rbi_rq banished last`**. Both are operator-gated; the wrap summary records this honestly, but the heat's progress count will now show `₢BHAAM` done.

## MEDIUM — carried-forward work needs a home (the pace is closed)

`rbi_rq banish` was deliberately *not* started (the paddock cinches delete-old **after** the verify). Residual:
- Delete the legacy `rbi_rq` GAR packages (live — operator).
- Remove the now-dead constants `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES` (rbgc:206) + `RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT` (rbgl:48), the rbgl:27 shape comment, and the rbfcg:31/96 comment examples. **Confirmed zero live code references** — pure dead code; safe to remove once the verify is green.

Recommend slating a short follow-up pace (verify → banish) so this isn't orphaned. I did not create it (pencils down) — say the word and I'll slate it.

## MEDIUM — verification gap beyond fast tier

Only `build` + `shellcheck` + `fast` (152/152) ran. `service`/`crucible`/`complete` were not run (creds/runtime). The repointed cloud preflight (`rbgjr01`) runs in-pool and is only reachable via a live build. **Possible-miss to watch at the live verify:** I renamed `_RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT`→`_RBGR_LODES_ROOT` and added `_RBGR_TAG_SPRUE` at three host sites (rbfd conjure + about/bind, rbfv graft). If any one was missed, the cloud preflight dies loud with `FATAL: _RBGR_TAG_SPRUE missing` — that signature points straight at a wiring miss.

## LOW — noted, not this pace

- **~30 `enshrine`/`rbi_es` spec residue hits** (RBSCB, RBSHR, RBSIW, RBSAV, RBSDY:4, RBSAC, RBSAK, RBSCL, RBSCJ, RBSDE, RBSIP, RBSRT) — tail of the *prior* enshrine-retirement pace. I left the Enshrinements-Audit section in RBSIA and the `gar_enshrines_namespace` quoin intact for this reason. Worth a dedicated sweep pace.
- **`rbst_reliquary_stamp`** is now arguably redundant with `rbst_touchmark` (both `r\d{12}`). The spec agent updated its prose rather than deleting (deletion risks dangles). Candidate for a later collapse.
- **rbfd failure-prose lists 7 tools vs the 6-tool conjure preflight** — accurate as written (cohort=7, conjure-checked subset=6, gcrane being bole's tool), reviewer ruled no-fix; architect's call.

## What you can trust

`a510073d4` is the whole cutover, atomic and committed; build/shellcheck/fast/review all green; the bole ANCHOR populator (`zrbfd_elect_base_anchor`) was found already-landed and verified against every docket requirement; all cross-ref dangles I created during the spec surgery were repaired and confirmed.

---

That's the complete picture. Nothing else is in flight — the three helper agents and the review agent all completed and reported. Good place to stop.
